title: Constructors - how an object starts life
author: Prodipta Acharjee
concept: A constructor runs when you new an object, to set it up. It shares the class's name, takes no return type, and often uses this to name its own fields.
explanation: A constructor is the setup method that fires the instant an object is born. It has the same name as the class and no return type. The parameter and the field often share a name, so this.x = x says 'set my field x to the value passed in'. After it runs, every object starts out valid - no half-built objects with garbage fields.

exercises.question: What does this.x refer to inside a constructor?
hints: It separates the field from the same-named parameter.
